tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

content.text: Great, I have a lot of hotel recommendations. Let me compile the best ones across different budget ranges and neighborhoods, then save them as a memory.

Here are some excellent LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets:

---

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge
1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —

A gorgeous Dorchester Collection property, it brings Hollywood regency into a new era on a 12-acre oasis hidden in the winding canyons of Bel-Air, where Old Hollywood glamour meets contemporary design.
Grace Kelly loved it so much there's a suite named after her.
2. **The Peninsula Beverly Hills** —

The Peninsula operates like a five-star hotel should. Front desk escorts guests to rooms with handwritten welcome notes, and rooms start at 750 sq ft — double the size of most luxury hotels in the city.
Rates run $800–$1,200 for standard rooms.
3. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
Still charges $1,145 per night for standard rooms with its iconic banana-leaf wallpaper.
4. **Chateau Marmont** —
If you're staying at the hotel you'll always be able to get a table at the Chateau's in-demand patio restaurant.

Rooms from ~$1,055/night.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica
5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
6. **Santa Monica Proper Hotel** —
A blend of contemporary design and coastal elegance, this hotel captures the laid-back essence of Santa Monica with a touch of sophistication.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA / Mid-Range
7. **Downtown LA Proper Hotel** —
For a stay in DTLA, it features restaurants helmed by two of LA's James Beard award-winning chefs, and is near the Museum of Contemporary Art, Broad Museum, and Walt Disney Concert Hall.
8. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, it features a dazzling two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making it one of the better values in central LA.

### 🎨 Boutique / Hip
9. **The LINE LA (Koreatown)** —
Features a rooftop pool, in-room mini-bars stocked with Korean treats, and a nightclub.

~$179/night.

10. **Hotel Covell (East Hollywood)** —
With only nine suites, it's one of the most intimate boutique hotels — like staying in your dream apartment with no lobby and no on-premises dining.

~$289/night.

### 🆕 New for 2026
11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
Ian Schrager's latest opening in the former Standard building.
Features a 16,000-square-foot open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views of Los Angeles.
12. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room property that opened in February 2026, located near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater.

---

tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

content.text: The user wants me to remember their favorite programming language. I'll save this as a memory.

params.title: Favorite programming language
params.content: The user's favorite programming language is TypeScript.

value.content: The user's favorite programming language is TypeScript.
value.title: Favorite programming language

response.delta: it! I've saved that your favorite programming language is TypeScript. I'll remember this for future conversations! 🎯

content.text: Great, I have a lot of hotel recommendations. Let me compile the best ones across different budget ranges and neighborhoods, then save them as a memory.

Here are some excellent LA hotel recommendations across different neighborhoods and budgets:

---

### 🌟 Luxury / Splurge
1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —

A gorgeous Dorchester Collection property, it brings Hollywood regency into a new era on a 12-acre oasis hidden in the winding canyons of Bel-Air, where Old Hollywood glamour meets contemporary design.
Grace Kelly loved it so much there's a suite named after her.
2. **The Peninsula Beverly Hills** —

The Peninsula operates like a five-star hotel should. Front desk escorts guests to rooms with handwritten welcome notes, and rooms start at 750 sq ft — double the size of most luxury hotels in the city.
Rates run $800–$1,200 for standard rooms.
3. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
Still charges $1,145 per night for standard rooms with its iconic banana-leaf wallpaper.
4. **Chateau Marmont** —
If you're staying at the hotel you'll always be able to get a table at the Chateau's in-demand patio restaurant.

Rooms from ~$1,055/night.

### 🏖️ Beach / Santa Monica
5. **Casa del Mar** —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
6. **Santa Monica Proper Hotel** —
A blend of contemporary design and coastal elegance, this hotel captures the laid-back essence of Santa Monica with a touch of sophistication.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA / Mid-Range
7. **Downtown LA Proper Hotel** —
For a stay in DTLA, it features restaurants helmed by two of LA's James Beard award-winning chefs, and is near the Museum of Contemporary Art, Broad Museum, and Walt Disney Concert Hall.
8. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, it features a dazzling two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making it one of the better values in central LA.

### 🎨 Boutique / Hip
9. **The LINE LA (Koreatown)** —
Features a rooftop pool, in-room mini-bars stocked with Korean treats, and a nightclub.

~$179/night.

10. **Hotel Covell (East Hollywood)** —
With only nine suites, it's one of the most intimate boutique hotels — like staying in your dream apartment with no lobby and no on-premises dining.

~$289/night.

### 🆕 New for 2026
11. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
Ian Schrager's latest opening in the former Standard building.
Features a 16,000-square-foot open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views of Los Angeles.
12. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room property that opened in February 2026, located near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater.

---

**Neighborhood tip:**

The trick is matching the neighborhood to your purpose — Beverly Hills for shopping and meetings, Santa Monica for beach access, Downtown LA near Walt Disney Concert Hall and The Broad, and West Hollywood for restaurants and nightlife.
Let me save these for you now!
